memories.memory_text: I remember the telex sliding across the table, six figures written plain—Lorenzo saying they wanted us for Buddy Israel, like it was some kind of honor. I said 'Bulllllshit...' 'cause my gut told me this was deeper than money.
grounded_facts: Lorenzo Wyman presented a bank telex offering six figures for an assassination job.
The target was identified as Buddy Israel.
Georgia Sykes reacted with skepticism, verbally expressing disbelief.
She asked for more information about what Padiche had said, indicating caution.

memories.memory_text: I set my fork down and wiped my hands—time to get serious. I needed to know the time frame, 'cause we weren't doing this half-assed.
grounded_facts: Georgia Sykes sets her fork down and wipes her hands on a napkin during the diner conversation.
Sykes asks 'What's the time frame?' to clarify the urgency and structure of the plan.

memories.memory_text: I sat there silent, just staring at Padiche after he pushed for details, letting him twist in the wind while Watters dropped that 'Pussy' bomb—he didn't even catch it at first, kept squinting like he misheard.
grounded_facts: Sykes remained non-responsive when Padiche asked for operational details.
Watters said 'Pussy' in a provocative manner, which Padiche initially misheard as 'Pushy'.
Sykes repeated 'Pussy' clearly after Watters, maintaining eye contact and control of the moment.

memories.memory_text: Watters looked at me like she wanted to eat me alive, saying 'Israel likes pussy'—I had to bite back the urge to flinch, just gave it back flat: 'And we like that he likes,' like it was nothing.
grounded_facts: Watters delivered the line 'Israel likes pussy' with a lewd sneer while looking almost lustfully at Sykes.
Sykes visibly squirmed but responded with a composed, deadpan line to deflect the intensity.
The exchange occurred in front of Padiche, who reacted with nervous confusion.

memories.scene_title: 39、INT. NOMAD HOTEL AND CASINO -- LOBBY -- MORNING
memories.memory_text: I saw those hookers come out all beat up, and when the clerk asked if we were sharing a room, I had to say fast—'No, we're not together'—before Watters could make it worse.
grounded_facts: Sykes arrives at the reception counter as Israel's Hookers exit the elevators, disheveled and injured.
The woman at the counter asks if Watters and Sykes need a room with two beds.
Sykes immediately responds, 'No, we're not together,' cutting off any assumption of intimacy.

memories.scene_title: 39、INT. NOMAD HOTEL AND CASINO -- LOBBY -- MORNING
memories.memory_text: Watters kept going on about those women like some righteous rant, and I could feel the clerk shutting down, so I just said, 'I'm gonna need a mini-bar key too,' to shut it off.
grounded_facts: Watters delivers a feminist diatribe about the hookers, criticizing their impact on women's image.
The hotel clerk remains silent, smiling stiffly, clearly uncomfortable.
Sykes interrupts by requesting a mini-bar key, redirecting the interaction.

memories.memory_text: I stashed the Glock, adjusted the earpiece after Watters complained about feedback—had to make sure it was clean. Told him we’re only hitting who’s in our way, not killing women just 'cause they’re caught up in this mess.
grounded_facts: Sykes is wearing a provocative outfit including a leather mini-skirt, lace garters, and thigh-high platform boots.
She stows a Glock 9mm and adjusts her earpiece/microphone combo after feedback is reported.
She explicitly states she does not want to kill women regardless of their occupation and limits targets to those directly obstructing access to Israel.

memories.memory_text: I pulled my small revolver from the garter holster, the skin on my thigh raw from the strap, leaning back in the elevator and waiting—this was the last push, no room for mistakes.
grounded_facts: Georgia Sykes retrieves a small revolver from her garter holster.
She rubs a chafed area on her body, indicating discomfort from the holster.
She is positioned in an elevator at the Horizon Hotel and Casino, preparing for action.

memories.memory_text: I asked Watters if she was gay—straight up—because the way we’d been locked in, intense and close, it made me wonder, and I don’t do circles when something’s pressing.
grounded_facts: Georgia Sykes directly questions Watters about her sexuality.
The question arises in a high-tension moment before an operation.
The interaction reflects Georgia’s pattern of cutting through ambiguity in personal dynamics.

memories.scene_title: 54、INT. NOMAD HOTEL AND CASINO -- LOBBY -- SAME
memories.memory_text: I saw those Tremor Brothers in the elevator, high and leering at me like I was meat—felt the bad vibes crawl up my spine. I didn't get on. Just said 'I'm going down,' and flipped Darwin off when he mouthed a blowjob.
grounded_facts: Georgia Sykes sees the Tremor Brothers in the elevator and decides not to enter.
She experiences a physical reaction ('bad vibes trickle up her back') upon seeing them.
She verbally states 'I'm going down' and makes a gesture (kissing her middle finger) in response to Darwin Tremor's lewd mimicry.
Darwin reacts to her insult with a scowl as the doors close.

memories.memory_text: I heard muffled gunshots over the earpiece—static, chaos—something went bad with Carruthers and Acosta down in the lift, and I knew right then the job just went sideways.
grounded_facts: Georgia Sykes is wearing an IFB earpiece and hears audio from another location.
She hears muted gunshots and static, interpreted as a violent confrontation between Carruthers and Acosta.
She reacts verbally with alarm, indicating she perceives the situation has deteriorated.

memories.memory_text: I heard another voice crack through my earpiece—somebody else was on our frequency, and I knew right then we were exposed.
grounded_facts: Sykes hears an unexpected transmission in her IFB (earpiece)
She reacts with disbelief
questioning the security of their communication channel

memories.memory_text: The elevator dinged and stopped at my floor—then Watters said security was locking it down, trapping me up here with no way out.
grounded_facts: Sykes sees the elevator reach her floor
She is informed the elevators are being locked down by security

memories.memory_text: I was pried at those elevator doors with my 9mm, usin' it like a crowbar—muscle in my good arm burnin’—just tryin’ to get through, not givin’ up yet.
grounded_facts: Sykes used her 9mm as a tool to force open the elevator doors.
She was physically straining with her functional arm during the attempt.
She expressed determination by saying, 'I'm not givin' it up jus' yet...'

memories.memory_text: The doors slid open on their own, and I stumbled back—smoke hit my face, cordite in the air, and that bone-saw sound... I just sat there, whisperin’ 'oh...damn...' like I couldn’t believe it.
grounded_facts: The elevator doors retracted automatically while Sykes was attempting to open them.
She lost her balance and fell onto the floor when the doors opened.
She perceived cordite, pistol smoke, and the sound of a bone-saw coming from inside the elevator.
She reacted with quiet shock, uttering '...oh...damn...' in a near whisper.

memories.memory_text: I recognized that guy—Hugo—right when he came through the stairwell, hands up. Something about the way he moved, the cut of his jacket... I knew he was a Fed before he even opened his mouth.
grounded_facts: Georgia Sykes identifies one of the intruders, Hugo, as an FBI agent upon his entrance through the stairwell.
This realization occurs suddenly, based on her observation of his appearance or demeanor.
She speaks the line 'Wait a minute...This one's a Fed' in the scene.

memories.memory_text: I patted Carruthers down and pulled his FBI creds—felt the card in my hand, knew it was real. Didn't even see the lights flicker back on.
grounded_facts: Georgia Sykes patted Carruthers down and obtained his FBI credentials.
She was holding the credentials in her hand.
She did not notice the elevator power being restored.

memories.memory_text: I kicked the bone saw out of his hand and leaned in close, patting him down even though he was slick with blood and barely hanging on—then I pulled that fake FBI ID and knew we’d been played by both of them.
grounded_facts: Sykes kicked the bone saw away from Acosta.
Sykes patted down Acosta while he was bloodied and barely conscious.
Sykes discovered Acosta's fake FBI ID identifying him as 'Gerald Diego: Federal Bureau of Investigation'.
Sykes verbally reacted to the discovery by stating 'Both these motherfuckers are FEDS.'

memories.memory_text: I keyed the IFB, let my voice cut through the static—told 'em I'd kill the one that ain't real, and I meant it.
grounded_facts: Georgia Sykes speaks over Messner's IFB earpiece with a threatening message.
She names two individuals—Gerald D and Donald Carruthers—implying she has them in her custody or sight.
Her transmission is initially distorted by static but briefly clears as the elevator rises.
She delivers the ultimatum: 'Cuz I'mma kill the one that ain't...'

memories.memory_text: I heard the elevator ding and pressed up against the wall, gun raised, waiting to see who stepped out.
grounded_facts: Georgia Sykes heard the elevator arrive.
She positioned herself against the wall in readiness.
She was holding her gun up and actively listening.

memories.memory_text: I heard Messner's voice crackle through the air and then saw Carruthers' IFB sitting in his lap—realized that's where the transmission was coming from.
grounded_facts: Sykes hears a transmission from Messner at the same time as seeing Carruthers' IFB in his lap.
She makes the connection that the IFB is the source of the audio.

memories.memory_text: I covered Carruthers' IFB and whispered into my own—tapped into the comms, told Shar I was pinned and calling in fire support on my count.
grounded_facts: Sykes physically covers Carruthers' IFB while speaking into her own device.
She transmits a message using tactical comms, declaring she is 'pinched' and requesting heavy support.

memories.memory_text: I was watching Acosta, but something outside the car pulled my focus—just for a second—and when I turned back, his hand was already sliding down toward his pant leg, reaching for that ankle piece.
grounded_facts: Sykes was monitoring Acosta in the elevator car.
Her attention was momentarily diverted by an external stimulus.
During that moment, Acosta began moving his hand toward his hidden ankle holster.

memories.memory_text: I'm in the elevator, hurt and trying to hit the panel to stop it, but I can't even get a response when I key the IFB—just dead air. I kept whispering into it, 'Baby, can you hear me?' like that'd make a difference.
grounded_facts: Sykes is wounded and inside an ascending elevator.
She attempts to reach the control panel to stop the elevator.
She uses her IFB to communicate, but it is non-functional.
She speaks into the IFB, asking if anyone can hear her.

memories.memory_text: I'm on my feet in the elevator, ribs on fire, kevlar gone, blood running down my stomach—doors open on the penthouse and I know it's go time.
grounded_facts: Georgia Sykes is standing in the elevator despite physical trauma.
She has discarded her kevlar vest.
She is wearing a black bra and mini-skirt.
There is blood dribbling over her midriff.
She has a deep indentation near her sternum with a large bruise, indicating blunt force trauma from a non-penetrating bullet.
The elevator arrives at the penthouse level and the doors open.

memories.memory_text: I stepped out the elevator, one hand against the wall to keep steady, blood slick on my skin, and put my Glock right on Darwin's head—told him drop that hammer or I drop him.
grounded_facts: Sykes exits the elevator injured, bleeding, and partially undressed.
She uses the wall to steady herself as she aims her gun at Darwin.
She verbally commands Darwin to drop the hammer, threatening to shoot.

memories.memory_text: I recognized him—same sick fuck from before—and told him he was 'bout to die by a killer motherfucker, just as the chopper hit the sky above us, the whole floor shaking, nobody moving.
grounded_facts: Sykes identifies Darwin as the man from an earlier encounter.
She states she knows 'a killer motherfucker' and that he will die by one.
The sound of an approaching helicopter grows louder, causing visible tension.

memories.memory_text: I stumbled toward Ivy, hurt bad, and he picked me up—carried me like I was nothing. I put Acosta’s fake badge in his hand so he’d know what to show them.
grounded_facts: Georgia Sykes was injured and unable to walk unassisted.
She handed Acosta's fake FBI credentials to Ivy as they fled.
Ivy carried her after she was shot.
They escaped through the exit while FBI agents were distracted.

memories.memory_text: I told him my real name—'Georgia'—and for a second, I let my guard down, lookin' up at him like some soft fool while he carried me down the stairs.
grounded_facts: Sykes reveals her name is Georgia during a moment of intimacy with Sir Ivy while being carried down the stairs.
She speaks in a shy, sweet tone and engages in flirtatious dialogue.
She allows Sir Ivy to see her in a vulnerable state, acknowledging her injuries and discussing their connection.

memories.memory_text: Messner stepped into the stairwell with his gun out, and I had mine under his chin before he even blinked—I told him we could live or die right there, and he chose to walk away.
grounded_facts: Sykes reacts immediately to Messner's appearance and armed threat by drawing her weapon.
She asserts control in the standoff, stating she didn't kill the FBI agent downstairs.
She gives Messner a choice to de-escalate, which he does by leaving the stairwell.

memories.memory_text: I was being carried out, looking up at him—safe, finally—and that moment of love and relief cut through everything else, even knowing Watters was watching.
grounded_facts: Georgia Sykes is alive and being carried by a man during the evacuation from the Horizon Hotel.
She shares a deeply affectionate gaze with the man carrying her.
The moment is observed by Watters through a sniper scope, though Sykes does not acknowledge this directly in the scene.

memories.memory_text: I remember—well, I was never supposed to exist. Born a bastard, right there in Vegas, after my mom got knocked up by some nobody hood.
grounded_facts: The narrator was born illegitimate, referred to as a 'bastard' in the scene.
The birth occurred in Las Vegas, following the mother's arrival.
The mother became pregnant two months after arriving in Vegas.

memories.memory_text: She came from Kansas City, chasing some dumb dream from a Dairy Queen job, ended up on the Vegas strip—same place they started calling me 'Aces.'
grounded_facts: The narrator's mother migrated from Kansas City to Las Vegas.
She previously worked at a Dairy Queen.
She moved to pursue a dream, described as a 'pipe dream.'
The nickname 'Aces' is tied to the narrator's origin in Vegas.

memories.memory_text: I was up on that stage, helping the magician with his act—cards, sleight of hand, the usual tricks—while Buddy sat in the back playing with a deck like it meant something more.
grounded_facts: Israel assisted a magician on stage at a second-rate dive bar.
The scene took place at night in a club setting.
Buddy was present in the lounge, sitting on a stripper's lap and handling a deck of cards.

memories.memory_text: I remember hunched over the Magician’s bed, fingers fumbling with the deck, trying to get the flick just right under his watchful eye—he didn’t say much, but I could feel it when I messed up.
grounded_facts: Young Israel performs card tricks for the Magician while he is in a full body cast.
The Magician is present and observing Israel’s practice.
Israel is actively laboring to perform, indicating focused effort.

memories.memory_text: Dupree fumbled the eight ball and started messing up fast. The cops got curious, began watching and writing things down. Soon, my actions brought the heat to Primo Sparazza.
grounded_facts: Dupree failed at the pool game, drawing attention.
Police began surveillance on the Carlotta organization.

memories.memory_text: I remember standing there with Sparazza at that casino opening, flashbulbs going off, both of us smiling like we were untouchable—him thinking I was loyal, me already counting the ways to take it all from him.
grounded_facts: Israel was present with Sparazza at a casino grand opening, photographed together under flashbulbs.
Israel is described as Sparazza's hand-picked protégé who secretly amassed loyalties to topple him.
The scene includes a montage of Israel building relationships with mob figures while betraying Sparazza.

memories.memory_text: I walked out after making bail and disappeared—nobody’s seen me since, and the bond expires in eighteen hours.
grounded_facts: Israel skipped bail after being released.
His bond is set to expire in eighteen hours.
He has not been seen since leaving after bail was posted by his law firm.

memories.memory_text: I'm holed up in the penthouse at the Nomad in Lake Tahoe under a fake name, and now Mecklen’s blabbing about it on the phone like it’s nothing—feds are already circling, set to meet me in twenty-four hours.
grounded_facts: Israel is staying in the penthouse level of the Nomad Hotel and Casino in Lake Tahoe, Nevada.
He is using an assumed name.
He is scheduled to meet with federal prosecutors within twenty-four hours.
His agent, Morris Mecklen, is communicating about his location and cooperation.

memories.memory_text: I remember the moment Locke said my lawyers sent Jack Dupree to pull me out—they were desperate to cover their own asses with the bond, but I knew the feds would never let that happen.
grounded_facts: Israel's legal firm, Culpepper, Brody and Reed, dispatched bondsman Jack Dupree to retrieve him after he skipped bail.
The bond amount was over three-quarters of a million dollars and set to expire soon.
Federal agents, including Locke, are determined to prevent Dupree from retrieving Israel.

memories.scene_title: 26、INT. PENTHOUSE SUITE -- NOMAD HOTEL & CASINO -- MORNING
memories.memory_text: I walked through the wreckage of last night, stepping around the passed-out girls like they were corpses—my robe caught on one of their wigs, tugged it loose, and I saw the dirt and scars underneath. I almost gagged, just let the robe fall instead of pulling it free and exposing her completely.
grounded_facts: Israel walks through the aftermath of a party
avoiding contact with unconscious prostitutes and debris. His bathrobe catches on a wig of one of the prostitutes
revealing her scalp with dandruff and scars. He reacts with physical disgust and lets the robe fall to avoid further dislodging the wig.

memories.scene_title: 26、INT. PENTHOUSE SUITE -- NOMAD HOTEL & CASINO -- MORNING
memories.memory_text: I was shuffling aces and dropping them on the bodies like they meant nothing, trying to steady my hands—Hugo came in gulping Mylanta, said he got the guy's machine, and I had to ask him twice, my skin crawling with impatience.
grounded_facts: Israel absently shuffles cards
pulling aces and placing them on the bodies of unconscious people. Hugo enters and reports reaching only the answering machine of the intended contact. Israel repeats his question
showing agitation and physical discomfort (fidgeting
rubbing nose).

memories.memory_text: I remember screaming at Ivy, right in his face, because he had the nerve to tell me to calm down after we got hit for fifty grand—like this was some minor setback instead of a disaster.
grounded_facts: Israel reacts furiously when Ivy tells him to calm down
Israel is enraged about a $50
financial loss
Israel gets physically close to Ivy during the confrontation

memories.memory_text: I remember barking orders—get the Russian to clean the place floor to ceiling, pack us up, replace the hookers, and call Mecklen—because this dump was compromised and reeking of failure.
grounded_facts: Israel orders Hugo to bring the Russian to clean the suite
Israel demands they be packed and relocated
Israel instructs sending out current prostitutes and getting new ones
Israel calls for an update from Mecklen

memories.memory_text: I remember the weight of the silence after he said I might be dead within a day — my chest tightened, but I still said I wasn't doing cocaine.
grounded_facts: Israel was confronted by Dr. Gill about cocaine use and potential imminent death due to cardiac distress.
Israel denied using cocaine despite the doctor's warning and the medical report.
Israel exhibited visible guilt and physical distress during the conversation.

memories.memory_text: I told Gill it wasn't possible for him to come, but he kept pushing, talking about coma and amputations like I didn't already know—like I needed him to scare me. Finally I gave in, said fine, come to Lake Tahoe, Hugo’ll meet you.
grounded_facts: Israel initially refuses Dr. Gill's offer to administer emergency treatment.
Dr. Gill warns Israel about the consequences of his condition, including coma and amputation.
Israel eventually agrees to let Dr. Gill come, providing instructions for him to fly to Lake Tahoe and be met by Hugo.

memories.memory_text: Right after hanging up, I walked to the bureau, rolled that hundred, and snorted one more line—told myself it was the last bit, just to get through, just to steady—felt it burn, same as always.
grounded_facts: Immediately after agreeing to medical help, Israel uses cocaine again.
He prepares the drug using a rolled hundred-dollar bill and snorts it from the bureau.
He speaks to himself during the act, framing it as a final dose before stopping.

memories.memory_text: I saw myself in the mirror—just for a second—and it hit me how far I've fallen, standing there while everything went to shit around me.
grounded_facts: Israel sees his reflection in a living room mirror after observing the chaos caused by the hookers.
He quietly says to himself, 'How the mighty have fallen,' indicating a moment of self-awareness and sorrow.
The scene immediately preceding the reflection involves physical violence and disorder among the women, which Israel watches with disdain.

memories.scene_title: 39、INT. NOMAD HOTEL AND CASINO -- LOBBY -- MORNING
memories.memory_text: I saw my Hookers come out of the elevator after the fight—bruised, barefoot, wigs crooked, slinging broken heels. They were loud, then got surrounded and taken away.
grounded_facts: Israel observed his associates, the Hookers, emerging from the elevator in a disheveled and injured state after a brawl.
The Hookers were barefoot, with broken heels, misaligned wigs, and damaged clothing.
They were verbally confrontational with security and were quickly surrounded and removed by hotel staff.

memories.scene_title: 41、INT. PENTHOUSE SUITE -- NOMAD HOTEL & CASINO -- MORNING
memories.memory_text: I was lying on that sawed-off baby grand, cards in hand, shuffling over and over while the coke sat ready—told Mecklen straight, if they didn’t give us no jail time in ten seconds, the deal was dead.
grounded_facts: Israel was lying on a baby-grand piano with sawed-off legs in the penthouse suite bedroom.
He was restlessly shuffling a deck of cards while cocaine was arranged on the piano.
He issued a verbal ultimatum to Mecklen, demanding no jail time for his associates or the deal would be terminated.

memories.scene_title: 42、INT. FOUR SEASONS HOTEL -- WASHINGTON D.C. -- DAY
memories.memory_text: I told Morrey I don’t have to do shit—no more cooperation—until I get what I want, and I meant it. These feds are pushing, but I’m the one taking the fall if this blows up.
grounded_facts: Israel refuses further cooperation with the feds unless his demands are met
He asserts control by emphasizing that he
not Morrey
bears the consequences if the deal fails
His statement occurs during a tense confrontation in the hotel room with Morrey and the feds present

memories.scene_title: 42、INT. FOUR SEASONS HOTEL -- WASHINGTON D.C. -- DAY
memories.memory_text: I saw the Joker when I pulled that card out of the air, and something just snapped—I said 'Alright...do it.' I knew it meant giving up Ivy, Beanie, all of them. Didn’t feel real, but I said it anyway.
grounded_facts: Israel performs a physical gesture of tension—tossing a deck of cards and catching one at random
The card he catches and reveals is the Joker
He then consents to the full betrayal of his team with the words 'Alright...do it'
The moment follows internal hesitation and is preceded by Morrey’s warning about consequences

memories.memory_text: I was packing my suits, shoving them into the bag, trying to focus on getting out, when Ivy asked about Mecklen and my body locked up—I couldn’t swallow right, like the lie was stuck in my throat.
grounded_facts: Israel is packing clothing into a garment bag in a disorganized way.
Ivy asks Israel about Mecklen.
Israel stiffens and shows visible signs of guilt when asked about Mecklen.

memories.memory_text: I saw the gun poking out of my bag and panicked, yanking the bedspread over it fast—then Ivy opened his coat and I saw the .50 Cal, and I couldn’t even look him in the eye.
grounded_facts: Israel notices a small handgun sticking out of his bag.
Israel covers the gun with the bedspread.
Ivy unbuttons his coat to reveal a .50 Cal in a shoulder rig.
Israel averts his eyes after seeing Ivy's weapon.

memories.memory_text: I pulled the cards from my pocket and shuffled them fast—left to right, right to left—making the movements sharp, hypnotic, showing Ivy exactly what I wanted him to see, nothing more.
grounded_facts: Israel removes a deck of cards from his robe pocket and begins shuffling them rapidly.
He performs card manipulations while speaking to Ivy, emphasizing control over perception.

memories.memory_text: I said the Feds could have what they wanted—something real, not just smoke and mirrors—and then whispered that I never wanted it this way, the Ace of Spades still hidden in my hand.
grounded_facts: Israel claims he can make illusions for the Feds that feel more real than the current moment.
He states 'I never wanted it this way' after asserting his value, while secretly holding the Ace of Spades in his hand.

memories.memory_text: I told him, 'You'll walk with me,' holding the Ace behind my back, feeling the edge press into my finger—right before I threw it.
grounded_facts: Israel says "You'll walk with me" with deliberate timing for effect.
Israel readies a playing card behind his back
slipping it between his index and middle finger.
The card is specifically an Ace
used as a concealed weapon.

memories.memory_text: I stepped hard and slung the Ace like a blade—it hit him square in the right eye, blood popping at the brow; he went down roaring.
grounded_facts: Israel throws the Ace of Spades with force
striking Sir Ivy in the right eye. The impact causes immediate bleeding at the browline. Sir Ivy collapses to the ground after the strike.

memories.memory_text: I was digging at my chest, trying to shake the numbness, and all I could think was that I had to make the call to Mecklen—before everything slipped away.
grounded_facts: Israel is in physical distress, with numbness in his limbs and chest pain.
He sets down a handgun and uses his cellphone to dial Morris Mecklen.
He speaks to Vitoli, telling him to wait while he handles the call.
Soot enters silently behind Israel, drawing a 9mm, but Israel does not react to him directly in the scene.

memories.memory_text: I was on the phone, clutching my chest, trying to breathe through the spasms when Mecklen said they revoked the deal—I couldn’t get air, just kept gasping, 'They what? What? No. No. Why?' like it might change anything.
grounded_facts: Israel is on the phone with Mecklen while experiencing chest spasms. Mecklen informs Israel that the deal has been revoked by the Deputy Director Locke. Israel responds with repeated disbelief and distress
physically struggling to breathe. The scene emphasizes Israel's acute cardiac distress coinciding with the news. Israel's dialogue is fragmented and panicked
indicating shock and physical deterioration.

memories.memory_text: I turned and saw that thing—Vitoli’s face torn apart, a gun pointed at my head, and then Doc Gill standing there like this was all under control—I barely got out 'Doc-- y-- wha-- what is this?' before my pulse went wild.
grounded_facts: Soot reveals his disfigured face and aims a 9mm at Israel's head. Israel becomes aware of Soot's presence and weapon only after turning around. Hugo and Doc Gill enter the suite moments before. Israel sees Gill and speaks to him directly
seeking explanation or help. His breathing tightens and pulse accelerates upon seeing the gun and Soot's face.

memories.scene_title: 122、INT. CEDARS SINAI -- I.C.U. -- RECOVERY ROOM -- DAY
memories.memory_text: I heard Locke say it—'They're father and son'—and suddenly everything about my mother, Sparazza, the way he looked at me, it snapped into place. I'm his. I'm actually his goddamn blood.
grounded_facts: Locke states that Israel and Sparazza are father and son
The revelation occurs while Israel is barely conscious in the ICU
The information includes that Israel is the product of a brief affair between Sparazza and Israel's mother, Laverne, in 1967

memories.scene_title: 122、INT. CEDARS SINAI -- I.C.U. -- RECOVERY ROOM -- DAY
memories.memory_text: I saw him looking at me through the tubes and wires, and I locked eyes with him—no words, just hate, raw and deep. I don't know if he felt it, but I meant every second of that glare.
grounded_facts: Israel and Sparazza exchange a glare of 'pure, unadulterated hatred' across the ICU room
Israel is barely conscious but visually aware of Sparazza
The moment occurs immediately after the paternity revelation

memories.memory_text: I adjusted the record level on the console just as the V-U meters spiked and began to modulate, making sure we captured Serna's voice clearly.
grounded_facts: Messner adjusted the record level
V-U meters spiked and modulated during the adjustment
Audio from Serna was being recorded at that moment

memories.memory_text: I tweaked the frequency on the digital console and keyed the mic to command the camera team—'Zoom and pan please. Stay tight.'—keeping the feed locked on the figure moving inside the mansion.
grounded_facts: Messner tweaked the frequency on a digital console
Messner used a microphone to issue a verbal command
The command was to adjust camera movement (zoom and pan)
The camera followed a figure inside the mansion

memories.memory_text: I remember questioning whether Heller was really a double agent—something didn't add up about his loyalty, and the way Locke paused before launching into that full history made me sure there was more beneath it.
grounded_facts: Messner questions Heller's status as a double agent during a briefing with Deputy Director Locke
The question prompts a detailed exposition on Heller's role in FBI undercover operations
The scene includes flashbacks to Heller's training and transformation, narrated by Locke

memories.memory_text: I remember the photos flashing on the screen—Sparazza with Sinatra, Israel at the casino opening, then late-night meetings with Sparazza’s men—and realizing he wasn’t just rising through the ranks, he was orchestrating a takeover that split the entire family apart.
grounded_facts: Sparazza was Israel's mentor and brought him into the mob.
Israel gained the loyalties of Sparazza's top men.
Israel's actions caused a civil war within the Carlotta organization.
The conflict divided the mob into two factions: Sparazza's and Israel's.
Multiple federal and state agencies launched investigations targeting Israel.

memories.memory_text: I asked if Acosta could be involved in the Israel hit—his history fits the kind of brutality we're seeing, and the idea that someone like him might be loose on a million-dollar contract unsettled me.
grounded_facts: Messner speaks aloud in the briefing room, directly questioning Acosta's possible involvement in the Israel hit.
Messner's line follows detailed descriptions of Acosta's violent past and mercenary status.
The context includes discussion of high-value bounties and multiple actors wanting Israel dead.

memories.memory_text: I told them it was the last place they'd look—Israel has to be intercepted before Dupree takes him back to Vegas, and I made sure they understood the weight of what's at stake.
grounded_facts: Messner states that the high-roller suite is 'the last place they'd look.'
Messner emphasizes the urgency of intercepting Buddy Israel before he is returned to Las Vegas by bondsman Jack Dupree.
Messner references the high operational and legal stakes involving Israel’s bond and his value as a witness.

memories.memory_text: I remember pulling off the headphones after hearing Serna mention the Swede—some supposed hitman with a million-dollar rate, but Carruthers said there's no record of him. I wondered if that meant he was just made up, or if he was really that good—never caught, no trace.
grounded_facts: Messner was listening to a surveillance tape where Serna mentioned the Swede.
Messner asked Carruthers about the Swede's credibility.
Carruthers reported no known Swedish hitman with such a profile.
Messner considered the possibility that the Swede's lack of record meant he was exceptionally skilled.

memories.memory_text: I flipped through the photos of Padiche and Serna again, thinking about how they're risking everything by moving directly against Sparazza—going to Israel, setting up a high-stakes play. The odds are long, and it felt like desperation, not strategy.
grounded_facts: Messner reviewed photographs of Victor Padiche and Sidney Serna.
Messner reflected on their plan to act against Sparazza.
He described their actions as a high-risk move with long odds.
He compared the situation to a gamble, questioning its viability.

memories.memory_text: I held up the file and counted thirty-six plastic surgeries—nose, eyes, chin, jaw redone over and over. I couldn't believe a man had been cut this much and we never knew.
grounded_facts: Messner reads from a file that Sparazza has had thirty-six major medical procedures since 1953
all elective plastic surgeries. He specifically notes multiple alterations to the nose
eyes
eyelids
chin
and jaw. He compares the extent of surgery to Hollywood actors
emphasizing disbelief. He physically turns the file toward Carruthers and points at the details.

memories.memory_text: I flipped to a photo paper-clipped in the file—Freeman Heller, murdered. I remember asking why the Bureau never moved on Sparazza for that, knowing he killed one of ours.
grounded_facts: Messner discovers a photograph of murdered Federal Agent Freeman Heller in the file. The photo is paper-clipped to the document

indicating it is part of the newly revealed evidence. He directly questions why the Bureau did not act against Sparazza after Heller's killing. His realization ties Sparazza to a high-profile agent's death
altering his perception of the target’s significance.

memories.memory_text: I remember demanding to know if the Justice lawyers had signed off before we moved, and when they hadn't, I still pushed to lock down the penthouse—Israel couldn't be allowed to flee, even without full clearance.
grounded_facts: Messner asked whether the Justice lawyers had approved the operation.
Messner inquired about the status of the sheriff's task force.
Messner decided to proceed with securing the penthouse despite lack of formal authorization.

memories.memory_text: I heard the deputy describe a handwritten receipt for maroon uniforms found on Jack Dupree’s body—no rental company name, just the color listed. That detail stood out immediately; it was too specific to be random.
grounded_facts: Messner asked 'What kind of uniforms?' after learning a receipt was found on the body.
The Sheriff Deputy reported the uniforms were listed as maroon in color on the receipt.
The receipt was found in Jack Dupree's pocket during the ME's processing.

memories.memory_text: I was on the phone with the deputy when Carruthers told me he was going to the Nomad. I said I’d take the car to the lake. We both plugged in our earpieces. I asked if he was going up to the Penthouse alone—he pulled his .45 and checked the chamber. Then Sykes’ voice came through.
grounded_facts: Messner was on the phone with the sheriff deputy and put the call on speaker.
Carruthers decided to go to the Nomad while Messner would go to the lake.
Both agents used IFB/earpiece comms and plugged them in.
Messner asked Carruthers if he was going up to the Penthouse alone.
Carruthers drew his .45 and checked the chamber while driving.
Sykes' voice came through the comms after Carruthers checked his weapon.

memories.memory_text: I held the evidence bag with the severed fingers, still not fully grasping what I was looking at—frostbitten, shot off—and only then did it hit me: Hollis Elmore, a known associate of Dupree, was now a victim.
grounded_facts: Messner is handed an evidence bag containing fingers
The Crime Scene Team Member identifies the fingers as belonging to Hollis Elmore
The fingers show signs of frostbite and were shot off
Hollis Elmore is confirmed as a known associate of Dupree

memories.memory_text: When the Task Force Chief said they found Dupree's rental car at the Nomad parking structure, I immediately asked where it was now—my voice tightened, sensing the situation was already slipping beyond containment.
grounded_facts: A Task Force Chief informs Messner that Dupree's rental car—a Dodge Stratus—has been recovered
The car was picked up at a commuter airport in Reno
Nevada
The car is currently located in the Nomad parking structure
Messner reacts with urgency
asking “Where's it at now?”

memories.scene_title: 68、INT. NOMAD HOTEL & CASINO -- PARKING STRUCTURE -- DAY
memories.memory_text: I arrived with the team, securing the parking structure and locking down access to the Penthouse level—feds already chaining the door shut behind us.
grounded_facts: Richard Messner arrives at the Nomad Hotel & Casino parking structure with law enforcement personnel.
The scene is cordoned off by Messner's team, asserting federal control.
Federal agents are seen chaining and bolting the door to the Penthouse level stairwell.

memories.memory_text: I saw the casino employee trembling, nails chewed raw, and when he said another agent had taken Carruthers—someone claiming to be from San Francisco with a Spanish name—I felt the air shift. The moment he said 'Garcia or Diego,' I knew it wasn't one of ours.
grounded_facts: Messner observed the casino employee's nervous behavior including gnawing fingernails and mangled cuticles
The employee reported an unknown agent claiming to be from San Francisco accompanied Carruthers onto the elevator
The imposter agent used a false name resembling 'Garcia' or 'Diego'
Messner immediately questioned the legitimacy of the agent upon hearing the name

memories.memory_text: When the agent confirmed the man wasn't from our office, I didn't hesitate—I ordered them to run the names through the D.C. database, my voice sharp over the lobby noise. The fact that he wore security attire and moved freely changed everything; we were no longer in control.
grounded_facts: An FBI agent confirmed the unknown individual was not from their office
Messner issued an order to run the suspect's claimed names through the D.C. database
The imposter was observed wearing hotel security attire and had access to restricted areas
Messner perceived a loss of operational control upon realizing the breach

memories.memory_text: I was in the elevator, trying to filter through the static on my IFB, when a broken transmission came through—Sykes saying there were two men, Gerald D and Donald Carruthers, and questioning which one was real.
grounded_facts: Messner hears a distorted transmission over his IFB earpiece while in the elevator
The transmission mentions 'Gerald D' and 'Donald Carruthers' and questions which one is real
Messner physically reacts by putting a finger in his ear and silencing the others in the car

memories.memory_text: For just a split second, the signal cleared—and I heard Sykes say, 'Cuz I'mma kill the one that ain't,' and I knew it wasn't a warning, it was a promise.
grounded_facts: The IFB signal briefly sharpens as the elevator passes the fourteenth floor
Messner clearly hears Sykes say, 'Cuz I'mma kill the one that ain't'
Messner immediately reacts by shouting 'STOP THE ELEVATOR!'

memories.memory_text: I positioned my team at the far end of the hallway, keeping the elevator in sight—every move had to be silent, controlled, ready for what was coming.
grounded_facts: Messner exits the elevator with a group of armed officers
The team spreads out and takes position at the far side of the hallway
They establish a clear line of sight to the open elevator at the opposite end
The environment is quiet, indicating a tactical, high-tension operation

memories.memory_text: I positioned the team in a two-man cover formation at the far end of the hallway, eyes on the open elevator—possible hostiles inside, ready for fire.
grounded_facts: Messner gives a tactical command to establish a two-man cover formation
The command is issued in a low voice, indicating controlled urgency
The elevator is open and perceived as a potential threat source
Messner alerts the team to watch for possible aggression or gunfire

memories.memory_text: I was pressing down on Carruthers' chest, again and again, trying to get his heart started—felt the bone give slightly under my hands, but nothing. He wasn't breathing.
grounded_facts: Messner is performing chest compressions on Carruthers in an attempt to restart his heart.
Carruthers is unresponsive and not breathing during the attempt.
The physical sensation of resistance and possible bone movement under Messner's hands is implied by the action.

memories.memory_text: I was pounding on Carruthers' chest, trying to bring him back, but he was gone—completely gone. I couldn't save him, and then I just broke, sobbing over his body.
grounded_facts: Messner attempted to revive Carruthers with chest compressions.
Carruthers was unresponsive and confirmed dead.
Messner sobbed while over Carruthers' body.

memories.memory_text: Something slipped from my pocket—Elmore's bag of severed fingers—and I didn't even remember keeping them. Looking at Carruthers' hand, missing fingers too, I almost laughed at how insane it all was.
grounded_facts: A bag containing Elmore's severed fingers fell from Messner's pocket.
Messner did not recall taking or holding onto the bag.
He observed Carruthers' severed hand, which was also missing fingers.
The parallel between the severed fingers triggered a near-laughing reaction rooted in absurdity.

memories.memory_text: I was unaccounted for after the operation went down—Locke had to order my location confirmed, and that phone call from Soot’s device was the only trace of me.
grounded_facts: Deputy Director Locke issued a direct order to account for Agent Messner's whereabouts.
Messner was not present or in contact during the immediate aftermath of the operation in the penthouse.
Locke used Soot's phone to attempt to locate or contact Messner.

memories.memory_text: I kicked open the stairwell door, adrenaline surging as I burst into the hallway—only to see the Swede stepping out of the elevator with Sparazza’s men, weapons drawn, at the exact same moment.
grounded_facts: Messner entered the penthouse floor hallway by force from the stairwell
The Swede and Sparazza's goons arrived via elevator simultaneously
Multiple armed individuals were present at the moment of convergence

memories.memory_text: I pointed at the Swede and demanded answers—'This is him? The hitman hired to kill Israel? He's a doctor?'—and Locke just stood there, touching my arm like that made any of it okay.
grounded_facts: Messner sees the Swede being addressed as Dr. Ingstrom and performing medical work on Israel
Messner questions Locke, expressing shock that the supposed hitman is actually a physician
Locke places a hand on Messner’s arm and guides him to a chair

memories.memory_text: Soot jumped out the window—just launched himself—and for a second he was suspended in the air, like some twisted piece of art against the sky, then he was gone. And nobody reacted. Nobody cared.
grounded_facts: Soot breaks free and jumps from the penthouse window
Messner observes Soot’s body hanging momentarily in the air before falling
No one else in the room reacts with alarm or concern

memories.memory_text: I pulled the plastic bag with the severed fingers from my pocket and held it out to Elmore, watching his face as he recognized what was inside.
grounded_facts: Messner retrieves a plastic bag containing three severed fingers from his pocket
He presents the bag to Elmore at the driver's side of the Barracuda
Elmore sees the contents
appears embarrassed
and places the bag on the seat

memories.memory_text: After seeing his bandaged hand and hearing him say he just wanted to go home, I offered to drive him—Las Vegas was his home, but he didn’t make it easy to help.
grounded_facts: Messner observes Elmore’s heavily bandaged left hand on the steering wheel
Elmore expresses desire to return to Las Vegas and forget the events
Messner offers to drive due to Elmore’s injury

memories.scene_title: 120、INT. CEDARS SINAI -- LAS VEGAS NEVADA -- DAY
memories.memory_text: I walked in clean, suit pressed, no tie, and flashed a badge that wasn't mine—just nodded when the orderly asked if I was with the third-floor group. One silent confirmation and I was through.
grounded_facts: Messner entered Cedar Sinai in a pressed suit without a tie.
He used a badge to pass the front desk.
An orderly questioned him about being part of a group on the third floor.
Messner responded by nodding, not speaking.
The orderly directed him to a stairwell leading to the ICU.

memories.memory_text: I pressed the gun into Locke's side and made him walk with me—he looked stunned, pale, like he couldn't believe I'd do it, but I needed answers now, not later.
grounded_facts: Messner draws a gun and presses it against Locke's ribs
Locke reacts with shock and is physically led away by Messner
This occurs in a hospital hallway crowded with federal personnel

memories.memory_text: I demanded to know why no one called us about Israel's situation being altered, why my partner's body was already being prepared for cremation while they covered up the truth.
grounded_facts: Messner confronts Locke about the lack of communication regarding Israel's status
He references his deceased partner's body being cremated the next day
He accuses the Bureau of withholding information about the operation

memories.scene_title: 122、INT. CEDARS SINAI -- I.C.U. -- RECOVERY ROOM -- DAY
memories.memory_text: I remembered the paternity suit from 1967—filed against Sparazza by Laverne, Israel’s mother. It clicked the moment Locke said they were father and son.
grounded_facts: Messner recalls a paternity suit from 1967 filed against Sparazza by Laverne, Israel's mother.
Locke reveals that Israel is Sparazza's son, which triggers Messner's memory of the legal record.
Messner connects the paternity suit to the current situation upon hearing the relationship.

memories.scene_title: 122、INT. CEDARS SINAI -- I.C.U. -- RECOVERY ROOM -- DAY
memories.memory_text: Locke started to say they figured out who Sparazza actually was—then stopped himself. I saw him realize he’d said too much.
grounded_facts: Locke begins to reveal that they discovered Sparazza's true identity but cuts himself off.
Locke experiences a moment of regret or caution after speaking, indicating a breach of confidentiality.
Messner observes Locke's slip and subsequent silence.
